title: fix: preserve trailing slash in service invocation method path
body:
## Description

Fixes #7686

When a client invokes a service using the `dapr-app-id` request header (or Basic Auth or the `/v1.0/invoke/` URL form), any trailing slash in the request path was silently stripped before being forwarded to the target app. This caused routes like `GET /api/events/2024-04-08T00:00:00/` to arrive as `GET /api/events/2024-04-08T00:00:00` — resulting in 404s on apps that treat the trailing slash as significant.

## Root cause

Go's `path.Clean` strips trailing slashes by design. It was used at three independent points in the invocation pipeline:

1. `findTargetIDAndMethod` in `pkg/api/http/directmessaging.go` — extracts method from header / Basic Auth / URL
2. `NormalizeMethod` in `pkg/messaging/method/normalize.go` — normalizes on every dispatch
3. `constructRequest` in `pkg/channel/http/http_channel.go` — defense-in-depth clean before building the outbound URL

## Fix

Introduce a `cleanPath` helper in each package that calls `path.Clean` but re-attaches the trailing slash when the original path ended with one:

```go
func cleanPath(p string) string {
    if p == "" {
        return p
    }
    hasTrailing := p[len(p)-1] == '/'
    cleaned := path.Clean(p)
    if hasTrailing && cleaned != "/" {
        cleaned += "/"
    }
    return cleaned
}
```

All three call sites are updated to use `cleanPath` instead of `path.Clean`, so a trailing slash survives end-to-end regardless of which entry point is used. Path traversal sequences (`../`) are still resolved correctly.

## Tests

- `TestFindTargetIDAndMethod`: 3 new cases — dapr-app-id header, Basic Auth, and `/v1.0/invoke/` URL all preserve trailing slash
- `TestNormalizeMethod`: 3 new cases — trailing slash preserved, trailing slash preserved after traversal resolution, root-only `/` stays empty string

comments.body: `cleanPath` will currently turn inputs that clean to "." or ".." into "./" or "../" when the original path had a trailing slash (e.g. "./" -> "./"). That can reintroduce dot-segments after normalization and can also create an explicit "../" prefix that later code tries to prevent.

The new behavior/tests expect a root-only method of "/" to normalize to an empty string, but `NormalizeMethod` currently returns "/" (since `path.Clean("/") == "/"`). Add an explicit check to map "/" to "".

`cleanPath` can currently convert inputs that clean to "." or ".." into "./" or "../" when the original string ends with '/'. That can reintroduce dot-segments (and even an explicit "../" prefix) into the outbound request path.

`cleanPath` can currently convert inputs that clean to "." or ".." into "./" or "../" when the original string ends with '/'. That can reintroduce dot-segments (and even an explicit "../" prefix) into the derived method path.

Fixed in commit 15b0908a — all 4 Copilot comments addressed:

1. **cleanPath dot-segment guard** (normalize.go, http_channel.go, directmessaging.go): added `&& cleaned != "." && cleaned != ".."` to the trailing-slash condition so inputs like `"./"` or `"../"` no longer have the slash re-appended after `path.Clean` resolves them to dot-segments.

2. **NormalizeMethod root-slash** (normalize.go): added `if cleaned == "/" { cleaned = "" }` after `cleanPath` so a root-only `"/"` method normalises to an empty string rather than being forwarded as-is.

files.patch:
@@ -100,6 +100,25 @@ func TestNormalizeMethod(t *testing.T) {
 		require.NoError(t, err)
 		assert.Equal(t, "api/v1/users", got)
 	})
+
+	// Trailing slash preservation (issue #7686)
+	t.Run("preserves trailing slash", func(t *testing.T) {
+		got, err := NormalizeMethod("foo/bar/")
+		require.NoError(t, err)
+		assert.Equal(t, "foo/bar/", got)
+	})
+
+	t.Run("preserves trailing slash with traversal resolved", func(t *testing.T) {
+		got, err := NormalizeMethod("admin/../public/")
+		require.NoError(t, err)
+		assert.Equal(t, "public/", got)
+	})
+
+	t.Run("root slash stays as empty", func(t *testing.T) {
+		got, err := NormalizeMethod("/")
+		require.NoError(t, err)
+		assert.Equal(t, "", got)
+	})
 }
 
 func TestValidateName(t *testing.T) {
